treatment_information.treatment_response: Patient developed spontaneous bruising and large hematomas

treatment_timeline.event: Surgical placement of a titanium rod to correct a fracture of the femur

treatment_timeline.date: One week after surgery in June 2006
treatment_timeline.event: Patient complained of pain in his right calf, DVT confirmed by Doppler ultrasonography
Anticoagulation started with heparin and Coumadin
Maintained on 9 mg Coumadin and 325 mg aspirin daily
Patient noticed spontaneous bruising and large hematomas
Referred for evaluation of life-long anticoagulation decision
Oral anticoagulation discontinued based on negative thrombophilia and normal Doppler ultrasonography

patient_outcome.final_status: Oral anticoagulation discontinued, normal blood flow in deep veins
patient_outcome.long_term_prognosis: Minimal calculated risk for DVT recurrence
patient_outcome.patient_perspective: Patient requested re-evaluation of anticoagulation due to bruising and hematomas
